sheet.title: ATS Changeover — 400A Mains / 250 kVA Standby Genset to MSB

items.rating: 400 A 4-pole ACB mains incomer, 50 kA Icu
400 A 4-pole ACB genset incomer, 50 kA Icu
400 A AC-1 4-pole mains contactor, coil 230 VAC
400 A AC-1 4-pole genset contactor, coil 230 VAC, mechanically interlocked with K1
Mains-fail detection — 3-phase UV relay, pickup 0.85×Un = 340 V, drop-out 0.7×Un, 27 ANSI
Mains-fail confirm delay 0.5 s on-delay (prevents nuisance transfer on dips)
Genset warm-up + transfer delay 10 s on-delay (matches genset start-time-to-stable)
Mains-restore retransfer hysteresis 60 s off-delay (mains must be stable for 60 s before retransfer)
TEST — manual genset start (black flush actuator, NO contact 13-14)
RESET — clear latched fault (yellow flush actuator, NO contact 13-14)
Mains incoming terminal block L1/L2/L3/N, 240 mm² DIN-rail
Genset incoming terminal block L1/L2/L3/N, 240 mm² DIN-rail
Outgoing common bus to MSB L1/L2/L3/N, 240 mm² DIN-rail
Control supply L/N 230 VAC + genset start command 24 VDC

connections.function: Mains L1 to mains incomer breaker pole 1
Mains L2 to Q1 pole 2
Mains L3 to Q1 pole 3
Mains N to Q1 pole 4 (4-pole switching)
Mains-side breaker output L1 to mains contactor K1 pole 1
Q1 pole 2 to K1 pole 2
Q1 pole 3 to K1 pole 3
Q1 N pole to K1 pole 4 (neutral)
Genset L1 to genset incomer breaker pole 1
Genset L2 to Q2 pole 2
Genset L3 to Q2 pole 3
Genset N to Q2 pole 4 (segregated genset neutral — TN-S separation)
Q2 output L1 to genset contactor K2 pole 1
Q2 pole 2 to K2 pole 2
Q2 pole 3 to K2 pole 3
Q2 N pole to K2 pole 4 (genset neutral switched separately)
K1 output L1 to common outgoing bus to MSB
K1 output L2 to common bus L2
K1 output L3 to common bus L3
K1 output N to common bus N
K2 output L1 to common bus L1 (K1/K2 interlocked — never simultaneous)
K2 output L2 to common bus L2
K2 output L3 to common bus L3
K2 output N to common bus N
Mains-fail UV relay K3 sense input L1
K3 sense input L2
K3 sense input L3
K3 sense neutral reference
K3 mains-fail output (NC, opens on mains-OK) energises KT1 on-delay timer
KT1 coil return to control N
KT1 output (after 0.5 s confirm) → 24 VDC genset start signal to genset controller
KT1 output also energises KT2 (10 s warm-up timer)
KT2 coil return to N
KT2 timed-out output opens K1 mains-contactor coil break path
KT2 NO contact (after 10 s) energises K2 genset contactor
K2 coil return to N
K3 mains-OK output (NO, closes on mains restored) → KT3 retransfer hysteresis timer
KT3 coil return to N
KT3 timed-out (60 s mains-stable) re-energises K1 mains contactor
K1 coil return to N
K1 NO auxiliary 13-14 drives H1 MAINS ON green lamp
H1 lamp return to N
K2 NO auxiliary 13-14 drives H2 GENSET ON amber lamp
H2 lamp return to N
S1 TEST push-button parallels KT1 start signal — manual genset exercise
S2 RESET clears H3 FAULT lamp latch (fault contact wired upstream of S2)

labels.text: K1/K2 mechanical interlock — both contactors cannot close simultaneously
Genset neutral switched separately from mains neutral (4-pole) — preserves TN-S separation between sources

sequence_of_operation: STEADY-STATE MAINS-OK: K3 senses 3-phase mains via X1, internal contacts 11/12 open (no mains-fail) and 11/14 closed (mains-OK). K1 mains contactor energised (held by KT3 timed-out path), K2 genset contactor de-energised. MSB drinks from common bus X3 fed by K1. H1 MAINS ON green lit. MAINS-FAIL DETECT: any phase or all phases drop below K3 pickup (0.85×Un = 340 V). K3 11/12 closes, K3 11/14 opens, KT3 retransfer timer de-energises. KT1 on-delay (0.5 s) starts — short confirm window rejects momentary dips. CONFIRM + GENSET START: at t = 0.5 s KT1 times out → KT1 15 closes → 24 VDC GEN-START signal to genset controller via X4 → genset crank/run sequence begins. Simultaneously KT2 (10 s on-delay) starts, modelling the worst-case genset start-to-stable interval. TRANSFER: at t = 10.5 s KT2 times out → KT2 15 opens K1 coil path (K1 drops out) → mechanical interlock allows K2 to close → KT2 18 closes → K2 coil energises → K2 main poles close → load on common bus X3 now fed from genset. H1 extinguishes, H2 GENSET ON amber lit. Total dead-time on the bus: ~50 ms contactor mechanical time. MAINS-RESTORE DETECT: mains voltage returns, K3 11/14 closes, K3 11/12 opens → KT3 (60 s off-delay) starts. The 60 s window confirms mains is stable and not just briefly recovered. RETRANSFER: at t = mains-restore + 60 s KT3 times out → KT3 15 closes K1 coil path → K2 drops out (KT2 path no longer asserted, K2 coil released as KT2 18 also reverts when mains-fail clears) → mechanical interlock releases → K1 picks up → load back on mains. H2 extinguishes, H1 lit. GENSET COOL-DOWN: handled by genset controller's own cool-down timer (typically 5 minutes after off-load); not part of this changeover scheme. TEST MODE: S1 momentarily provides manual GEN-START signal (parallels KT1 output) for periodic exercise without simulating mains-fail. FAULT LATCH: any persistent genset failure (fail-to-start, low oil pressure, high coolant temp) latches H3 FAULT red via genset controller alarm contact; S2 RESET clears the latch.

assumptions: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 560 (safety services) applies to the ATS as a switching scheme between the public mains and a private standby generator; § 537 covers isolation and switching, § 314.1 covers division of installations into circuits.
Upstream rating consumed from db-layout-rollup (MSB main switch 400 A) — see meta.consumed_intents[].

K1/K2 are AC-1 utilisation category contactors selected for steady-state currents — ACB feeder breakers Q1/Q2 deliver overcurrent and short-circuit protection; contactor short-time withstand verified against the 16 kA declared fault level at the changeover incoming point.

Mains-fail confirm 0.5 s rejects momentary dips and starts/stop ratings; transfer time 10 s aligns with the declared genset start-time-to-stable of 10 s; retransfer 60 s prevents flicker-trip on partially-restored mains.

4-pole switching mandated: TN-S earthing system has segregated genset neutral, and 4-pole break is required when the genset has a separately-derived neutral-earth bond on its alternator star point to avoid parallel neutral-paths under retransfer.
No fault-level intent consumed; the 16 kA prospective fault current at the ATS incoming is engineer-declared via inputs.json.
No earthing intent consumed; the TN-S earthing system + genset star-point bond strategy is engineer-declared via inputs.json.

rationale.chat_summary: ATS changeover scheme for UK commercial premises: 400 A mains incomer + 250 kVA standby diesel genset to MSB. K1/K2 4-pole AC-1 contactors with mechanical interlock; K3 UV relay (ANSI 27) for mains-fail detection; KT1 0.5 s confirm + KT2 10 s genset warm-up + KT3 60 s retransfer hysteresis. Consumes db-layout-rollup (MSB main switch 400 A). Reply to refine (e.g. add load-shedding contactor for non-essential circuits).

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: control_changeover (ATS_4P topology) on sheet SHT-002 for a UK commercial premises. The schematic governs source-switching between a 400 A 3-phase mains incomer and a 250 kVA standby diesel generator (360 A FLA) feeding a common outgoing bus to the MSB. 4-pole switching is selected over 3-pole because the genset alternator has a separately-derived neutral-earth star-point bond; under retransfer a 3-pole break would leave a parallel neutral path between the mains and genset earthing systems. The schematic consumes one intent: db-layout-rollup from the parent db-layout skill, supplying the MSB main switch rating (400 A) which sizes the contactor + breaker selection.

decisions.label: ATS topology, 4-pole switching
decisions.summary: Standby genset has separately-derived neutral — 4-pole break prevents parallel neutral path under retransfer.
decisions.code_clause: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 560.6 (safety services switching) + BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 551.4 (generator parallel operation exclusions)

decisions.label: Upstream rating consumed from db-layout-rollup
decisions.summary: MSB main switch rating 400 A from db-layout-rollup sizes K1/K2 contactor selection.

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: Mains source: 400 V 3-phase + N, 400 A nominal, 16 kA prospective fault current at the ATS incoming (engineer-declared — no fault-level intent), TN-S earthing system. Genset source: 250 kVA / 400 V / 360 A FLA diesel set with engine-start time-to-stable of 10 s (manufacturer datasheet typical for a 250 kVA Stage V engine). The genset has a separately-derived neutral-earth bond at its alternator star point, which establishes a parallel local earthing system during genset operation and mandates 4-pole switching at the ATS. db-layout-rollup intent supplied the MSB main switch rating (400 A) that drives K1/K2 + Q1/Q2 sizing.

decisions.label: 16 kA prospective fault current at incoming engineer-declared
decisions.summary: No fault-level intent — value engineer-declared, K1/K2 short-time withstand verified against 16 kA.

decisions.label: Genset star-point neutral bond engineer-declared
decisions.summary: No earthing intent — TN-S + genset separately-derived neutral declared in inputs.json.

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 17 devices placed across power, control, and indication. Power: Q1 + Q2 (400 A 4-pole ACB incoming breakers, 50 kA Icu covering the 16 kA prospective fault with healthy headroom), K1 + K2 (400 A 4-pole AC-1 contactors, mechanically interlocked — the canonical ATS arrangement). Control: K3 (3-phase UV relay ANSI 27 for mains-fail detect, pickup 0.85×Un = 340 V), KT1 (0.5 s on-delay confirm timer to reject dips), KT2 (10 s on-delay matched to genset start-to-stable), KT3 (60 s off-delay retransfer hysteresis). Indication: H1 green MAINS ON + H2 amber GENSET ON + H3 red FAULT. Push-buttons: S1 TEST (manual genset exercise), S2 RESET (clear fault latch). 4 terminal rails X1-X4 segregate mains-in / genset-in / common-out / control-supply.

decisions.label: K1/K2 AC-1 contactors with mechanical interlock
decisions.summary: AC-1 is correct for switching steady-state distribution currents — ACB Q1/Q2 deliver fault clearance; mechanical interlock guarantees the K1/K2 NO/NO pair cannot close simultaneously.
decisions.rule: Contactor utilisation category + interlock requirement for ATS
decisions.code_clause: BS EN 60947-4-1 § 4.4.2 (AC-1) + BS EN 60947-6-1 § 6 (transfer switching equipment)

decisions.label: K3 ANSI 27 UV relay for mains-fail detect
decisions.summary: Pickup 0.85×Un / drop-out 0.7×Un follows BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 445 undervoltage protection bands; KT1 0.5 s confirm rejects momentary supply dips.

decisions.label: Q1/Q2 ACBs 400 A / 50 kA Icu
decisions.summary: 50 kA Icu provides >3× headroom over the 16 kA prospective fault — meets BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 434.5.1 breaking-capacity requirement with margin.

sections.title: Connection Topology
sections.summary: Power: 2 parallel 4-pole 240 mm² paths (W1-W24) — mains X1 → Q1 → K1 → X3 and genset X2 → Q2 → K2 → X3. K1/K2 outputs paralleled onto common bus X3 with mechanical interlock guaranteeing mutual exclusivity. Control: K3 senses 3-phase mains (W25-W28); K3 11/12 NC drives KT1 on mains-fail; KT1 15 gates the 24 VDC GEN-START to the genset controller (W31) AND energises KT2 (W32). KT2 15 opens K1 coil (W34), KT2 18 energises K2 (W35). On mains-restore, K3 11/14 energises KT3 (W37); KT3 15 re-energises K1 (W39) after 60 s stable confirm. Indication: K1 aux drives H1, K2 aux drives H2. 4-pole switching of neutral (W4/W8/W12/W16/W20/W24) prevents parallel N path between mains TN-S and genset star-point bond.

decisions.label: K1/K2 outputs paralleled with mechanical interlock
decisions.summary: Both K1 and K2 outputs land on common bus X3 — interlock prevents back-feed.

decisions.label: 4-pole neutral switching across both sources
decisions.summary: W4/W8/W12/W16/W20/W24 carry switched neutral — prevents parallel N path under retransfer between mains TN-S and genset separately-derived neutral.

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 560 (safety services), § 537 (isolation and switching), § 551.4 (generator parallel operation exclusions), § 445 (undervoltage protection), § 434.5.1 (breaking capacity), BS EN 60947-4-1 (contactors), BS EN 60947-6-1 (transfer switching equipment), BS EN 61082-1:2014 (documentation), and IEC 60255-127 (UV relays). No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Two partial-intent assumptions documented: fault-level (16 kA engineer-declared) and earthing (TN-S + genset star-point engineer-declared) — both flagged in compliance_summary.assumptions[].

decisions.label: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 primary citation form
decisions.summary: Operative regulation cited (e.g. § 560.6, not § 560 alone); amendment +A2:2022 included per Sprint 3-W2c citation discipline.

decisions.label: BS EN 60947-6-1 for ATS-specific compliance
decisions.summary: BS EN 60947-6-1 is the dedicated standard for transfer switching equipment (TSE) — cited directly for K1/K2 + mechanical interlock requirements.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Hybrid-mode: db-layout-rollup consumed (MSB main switch 400 A); fault-level + earthing absent — values engineer-declared in inputs.json and re-stated in compliance_summary.assumptions[]. Genset cool-down (typically 5 min after off-load) is handled by the genset controller's own logic, not by this changeover. Load-shedding contactor for non-essentials is not specified — the 250 kVA set is sized for full MSB load, but a future revision dropping genset size would add a non-essential shed contactor in series with K2 output, gated by genset-load-monitor. Fault latch H3 RED is driven by the genset controller's alarm contact (fail-to-start, low oil, high coolant) via X4-FAULT; S2 RESET clears the latch.

decisions.label: Hybrid-mode: db-layout-rollup consumed, fault-level + earthing absent
decisions.summary: One intent consumed; two partial-intent assumptions disclosed.
decisions.rule: Hybrid-mode disclosure per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Cross-Skill Cascade Verification
sections.summary: db-layout-rollup → schematic: MSB main switch rating (400 A) drives the K1/K2 contactor + Q1/Q2 breaker selection on this schematic. Cascade is self-consistent: 400 A MSB main switch ↔ 400 A K1/K2 AC-1 contactors ↔ 400 A 50 kA Q1/Q2 ACBs ↔ 240 mm² Cu busbar conductor (12 connections W1-W24). Fault-level intent not consumed — when a future revision adds fault-level, the schematic should be regenerated to cross-check Q1/Q2 Icu against the cascade-computed value rather than the engineer-declared 16 kA.

decisions.label: MSB main switch 400 A from db-layout-rollup → K1/K2 sizing
decisions.summary: Single-intent cascade self-consistent at 400 A across the ATS.

invariants.evidence: schematic_type ↔ items consistency: schematic_type='control' is satisfied by the items[] population (ATS controller, sensing relays, indicating lamps, sequence interlocks) which align with the control-schematic item-type whitelist.

Connection topology valid: every connections[*].from_item_id and to_item_id resolves to an items[*].item_id (verified by id-set membership). No dangling endpoints. Sequence_of_operation references only items present in items[].
Symbol resolution per BS EN 60617: every item carrying a symbol_ref maps to a documented symbol id in shared/symbols/bs-en-60617-symbols.json (graphic-12-04 'contactor', graphic-07-15 'timer', etc.).
